task2_architectureAudit.whyAllShare100g: Architecture uses category-level estimatedQuantity per PRODUCE_CONVERSION_HINTS entry, not per-product weights. Fresh herbs group assigns one value (100g) to all tokens in the array.
task2_architectureAudit.oneBunchEquals100g: Code maps mo/bunch purchases through detectConversionHint → estimated_quantity 100 → resolveUsablePerPricedUnit 100g per priced bunch → €/kg operational
task2_architectureAudit.notIndividualWeights: No herb-specific weight table; Hortelã on VL is kg-priced but still matches HORTELA token at 100g hint (parallel path)
evidence: ingredient-unit-inference.ts L412-417: single fresh herbs entry, estimatedQuantity: 100
stock-normalization.ts L1690-1695: conversion_hint multiplies purchaseQuantity × estimated_quantity
invoice-purchase-format.test.ts L519-529: leafy parallel — estimated_yield for ALFACE at group 500g

tomilhoDbUntilReread.note: ingredients row persists purchase_quantity=1, base_unit=un until invoice re-read

sideEffects: detectConversionHint('Tomilho') would return fresh herbs 100g (same as siblings)
operational display €20.60/kg at €2.06/bunch (identical math to Manjericão)
recipe lines in g would cost qty × €0.0206/g vs current null for g
recipe lines in un would remain N × €2.06 if cost_base_unit stays un until persistence refresh

tomilhoAlecrimEstragao.note: ALECRIM and ESTRAGAO contribute 0 VL rows

hundredGramOrigin.question: Is 100g: A) generic herb assumption B) product-specific C) restaurant assumption D) arbitrary placeholder?

A_genericHerbAssumption: Single 'fresh herbs' group with one estimatedQuantity: 100 shared by all 5 tokens
No per-token weight differentiation in code
detectConversionHint reason: 'fresh herbs token X → estimated 100g usable'
Parallel leafy produce group uses single 500g for 7 tokens
B_productSpecific: No product-specific weights; Manjericão and Hortelã share same 100g despite different VL purchase units (mo vs kg)
C_restaurantAssumption: No restaurant-specific yield data, supplier spec, or VL weighing evidence in code or commits
D_arbitraryPlaceholder: no test pins 100g to measured bunch weight
introduced without per-herb justification in commit message

finalVerdict.question: Is 100g for Tomilho consistent with existing herb-conversion architecture?
finalVerdict.answer: Adding TOMILHO at 100g matches the existing fresh-herb group design: one shared estimatedQuantity per label, same pipeline as MANJERICAO. Tomilho is the only VL bunch herb missing the token; €20.60/kg and recipe denominator 100g follow Manjericão math at identical €2.06/bunch.
finalVerdict.safetyNote: Code-token addition alone does not rewrite persisted ingredients row (purchase_quantity=1, base_unit=un) until re-read; 0 VL recipe_ingredients rows for Tomilho today.
